_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-12R Albuterol (Revised 12/10/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2026). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-12R: Albuterol — MDHHS Approved 1/5/2026, Reviewed 2026
indications: Known or suspected hyperkalemia resulting from a crush injury
precautions: Tachycardia is expected — monitor HR; caution in patients with underlying cardiac dysrhythmia
May worsen tremor in patients with Parkinson's disease or essential tremor
Dosing is not strictly weight or age-based — reference MI-MEDIC for pediatric patients
expected_effects: Bronchodilation within 5-10 minutes of nebulized treatment
notes: Standard dose: 2.5 mg in 3 mL NS nebulized over ~8-10 minutes
Crush injury protocol: 2.5 mg/3 mL NS continuous nebulization to a maximum dose of 20 mg
A single responding unit is not expected to carry 20 mg (8 nebules); the 20 mg maximum applies when additional resources such as a HazMat drug box or second drug box are available
Pediatric dosing: not strictly weight-based; reference MI-MEDIC for pediatric guidance
May combine with ipratropium bromide (9-28R) as combined 'DuoNeb' nebulization for continued bronchospasm

dosing.notes: Standard nebulized treatment over ~8-10 minutes; may repeat per treatment protocol
Reference MI-MEDIC for pediatric dosing guidance; nebulized over ~8-10 minutes

dosing.dose: 2.5 mg/3 mL NS continuous nebulization to maximum 20 mg
dosing.notes: For suspected hyperkalemia in crush injury; maximum dose depends on available resources

name: Albuterol Sulfate

setup: Place 3 mL unit-dose vial contents (2.5 mg albuterol in 3 mL NS) in lower half of nebulizer chamber
Assemble nebulizer unit and attach to T piece with mouthpiece or pediatric/NRB mask
Connect oxygen tubing from nebulizer to oxygen source
Set oxygen flow at 6 LPM as driving gas
Instruct patient to breathe normally, with deep inspiration every 4–5 breaths
Continue until all medication delivered; tap reservoir gently to re-disperse if needed
administration.pediatric_note: For small children, use blow-by or pediatric mask if unable to use mouthpiece
administration.post_administration: Obtain complete reassessment: vital signs and lung sounds after treatment completion

six_rights_for_this_drug.verification: Patient has wheezing/bronchospasm; or confirmed hyperkalemia from crush injury
Albuterol Sulfate — label reads 'Albuterol Sulfate 2.5 mg / 3 mL' — verify label matches verbalization
2.5 mg / 3 mL NS — NOT weight/age based for pediatric respiratory distress; same dose as adult
SVN nebulized — O₂ at 6 LPM as driving gas; appropriate mask or mouthpiece selected
Baseline vitals and lung sounds obtained; no known hypersensitivity; patient can tolerate mask
Document: drug name, dose (2.5 mg), route (SVN), time, justification (protocol), cross-check, and patient response post-treatment
